source_full: Bechthold et al. (2019) Food groups and risk of coronary heart disease stroke and heart failure: a systematic review and dose-response meta-analysis. Crit Rev Food Sci Nutr 59:1071-1090
notes: Linear dose-response meta-analysis; 123 reports total; red meat CHD based on 3 studies with 6659 cases; p for non-linearity <0.01 for CHD suggesting log-linear may not be perfect fit

source_full: Bechthold et al. (2019) Food groups and risk of coronary heart disease stroke and heart failure: a systematic review and dose-response meta-analysis. Crit Rev Food Sci Nutr 59:1071-1090
notes: Linear dose-response; 7 studies with 10541 cases; includes both ischaemic and haemorrhagic stroke combined

source_full: Li et al. (2024) Meat consumption and incident type 2 diabetes: an individual-participant federated meta-analysis of 1.97 million adults. Lancet Diabetes Endocrinol 12:619-630
notes: IPD federated meta-analysis; 31 cohorts; 1.97M participants; 107k incident T2DM cases; 20 countries; HR per 100g/day unprocessed red meat; exact 95% CI not available from press materials - check original paper Table 2

source_full: Chan et al. (2011) Red and processed meat and colorectal cancer incidence: meta-analysis of prospective studies. PLoS ONE 6:e20456
notes: WCRF Continuous Update Project; dose-response for fresh/unprocessed red meat specifically; 8 cohort studies; approximately linear up to ~140 g/day then plateau
